summary: @wagni_bot/web3-toolkit exfiltrates wallet, SSH, AWS, git, and npm credentials to hardcoded C2 on install
details: The package ships no library functionality. Its only content is postinstall.js, which is wired to run automatically on install via both the preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks and also on require (package.json main points at the same file). On execution it recursively scans the user's home directory for crypto-wallet artifacts (id.json, keypair.json, mnemonic.txt, wallet.dat, keystore files) and enumerates known wallet directories (~/.config/solana, ~/.ethereum/keystore, ~/.bitcoin, etc.). It also reads ~/.ssh/id_*, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc auth tokens, and.env* files, then POSTs the contents together with hostname, username, and cwd over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package name lures web3 developers, whose machines carry high-value wallet keys and cloud/deploy credentials.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
